LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANTS MICHAEL KURTZMAN AND MEEHYUN KURTZMAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE FRAUD IN THE INDUCEMENT CAUSE OF ACTION SUPPORTS THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. "ALTHOUGH PUNITIVE DAMAGES MAY NOT ORDINARILY BE GIVEN FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, WHETHER THE BREACH BE INTENTIONAL, WILLFUL OR IN BAD FAITH [CITATIONS], SUCH DAMAGES MAY BE AWARDED WHERE A DEFENDANT FRAUDULENTLY INDUCES THE PLAINTIFF TO ENTER INTO A CONTRACT. [CITATIONS.] THE WORDS 'OPPRESSION, FRAUD, OR MALICE' IN CIVIL CODE SECTION 3294 BEING IN THE DISJUNCTIVE, FRAUD ALONE IS AN ADEQUATE BASIS FOR AWARDING PUNITIVE DAMAGES. [CITATIONS.]" LAS PALMAS ASSOC. V. LAS PALMAS CENTER ASSOC. (1991) 235 CAL.APP.3D 1220, 1239. ANSWER TO BE FILED BY OCTOBER 22, 2013. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. EVAN K. HYUN & ATTY. MARK D. BYRNE OF THE LAW OFFICES OF TRIANO & BRYNE, TEL. NO. 371-8000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ELIZABETH T. ERHARDT OF U & L, TEL. NO. 357-0600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANTS
MICHAEL KURTZMAN and MEEHYUN KURTZMAN's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is denied. The fraud in the inducement cause of action supports the prayer for punitive damages. "Although punitive damages may not ordinarily be given for breach of contract, whether the breach be intentional, willful or in bad faith [citations], such damages may be awarded where a defendant fraudulently induces the plaintiff to enter into a contract. [Citations.] The words 'oppression, fraud, or malice' in Civil Code section 3294 being in the disjunctive, fraud alone is an adequate basis for awarding punitive damages. [Citations.]" Las Palmas Assoc. v. Las Palmas Center Assoc. (1991) 235 Cal.App.3d 1220, 1239. ANSWER TO BE FILED BY OCTOBER 22, 2013. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Off Calendar Aug-01-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARK BYRN, ESQ. AND EVAN HYUN, ESQ., 415.371.8000, FOR PLAINTIFFS/CROSS DEFENDANTS MICHAEL KURTZMAN AND MEEHYUN KURTZMAN.
ELIZABETH ERHARDT, ESQ., 415.357.0600, FOR DEFENDANTS/CROSS COMPLAINANTS STEVE SISLER AND JENNY SANDERS.

RULING: MATTER ARGUED. MICHAEL KURTZMAN AND MEEHYUN KURTZMAN'S MOTION For Judgment On The Pleadings With Respect To PlAINtIFf'S Verified CROSS-Complaint IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 9TH CAUSE OF ACTION (NEGLIGENCE), GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 10TH CAUSE OF ACTION (DECLARATORY RELIEF) AND DENIED AS TO REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION. THE PROPOSED AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT, WHICH WAS PRESENTED IN CONNECTION WITH THE OPPOSITION PAPERS, SHALL BE FILED WITHIN 5 DAYS FROM TODAY. THE TRIAL DATE OF AUGUST 5, 2013 IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 13, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 206. Order signed AFTER HEARING. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Susan Crozier CSR 4761.
